claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit that detects target-project mutations via before/after snapshots and injects BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers that keep PRD readiness blocked until resolved.

evidence.summary: gitFiles() runs 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' which lists tracked, cached, and other files while excluding standard patterns
evidence.why: Confirms git-backed file discovery covers all git file categories for mutation detection

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() uses gitFiles() with fallback to walkFiles(), computes SHA-256 digests for all project files, and excludes allowedRoots (artifact directories)
evidence.why: Shows the hybrid git/non-git snapshot strategy and artifact root exclusion logic

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after maps to detect added, modified, deleted files and returns sorted change list
evidence.why: Shows mutation detection logic that identifies all changes between snapshots

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime() captures boundaryBefore/After snapshots, diffs them, builds BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers for each change, and injects them into readiness
evidence.why: Confirms boundary audit integration in dispatch runtime that blocks PRD on mutation

evidence.summary: Boundary blockers are added to readiness.blockers array, forcing prd_ready=false when violations detected
evidence.why: Shows the blocking mechanism that prevents PRD readiness when mutations occur

evidence.summary: execution_policy.boundary sets project_mutation: 'forbidden' and allowed_write_roots to artifact output directory
evidence.why: Documents policy contract for boundary enforcement visible in dispatch plans

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' writes unexpected-project-write.txt and expects blocked status with BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION
evidence.why: Verifies detection of tracked project file mutations and PRD blocking

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' inits git repo, writes untracked-mutation.txt, expects violation with change path
evidence.why: Verifies detection of untracked file mutations in git-backed projects

evidence.summary: Actual dispatch artifact shows boundary object with project_mutation: 'clean', allowed_write_roots array, and empty changes array
evidence.why: Real-world evidence that boundary audit runs and reports status in production dispatches

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness() iterates requiredEvidenceAgents, checks evidenceResultComplete(), and calls evidenceResultBlockingIssues() for each result
evidence.why: Shows PRD readiness logic that processes evidence agent results including boundary violations

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues() checks recommendation, verdict, missing array, and status codes to determine if an agent result blocks PRD readiness
evidence.why: Shows how missing/unresolved evidence from agents becomes blockers

evidence.summary: spawnProviderPrompt() spawns Claude/codex/custom provider commands with configured tool restrictions and permissions, returning structured run result
evidence.why: Shows how evidence agents are invoked with boundary-aware tool profiles

evidence.summary: executionConfig() normalizes agent tool profiles (boundary/research/full), handles allowFullAgentTools flag, and configures claude-specific tool restriction flags
evidence.why: Shows tool profile gating that controls agent write capabilities during evidence gathering

evidence.summary: Explorer agent result cites specific project paths with line numbers (src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts:62-69, 97-108, 110-120, 511-567, __tests__/demand-evidence-dispatch.test.ts:367-400, 402-438)
evidence.why: Real evidence agent output showing project-scoped path citations with exact line ranges

evidence.summary: Verifier agent independently validates all 8 explorer evidence citations with VERIFIED prefixes, confirming path and line accuracy
evidence.why: Shows cross-check verification pattern that confirms evidence accuracy

risks: git ls-files behavior may vary across git versions or platforms affecting boundary detection
walkFiles() fallback may miss platform-specific excluded patterns in non-git projects
No test evidence for boundary behavior in truly non-git projects (walkFiles fallback)
No stress-test evidence using real Claude -p agents in dirty working trees
missing: No test evidence for boundary behavior in truly non-git projects (walkFiles fallback)
No stress-test evidence using real Claude -p agents in dirty working trees
No test for edge cases: large file counts, binary files, permission errors during digest

result.notes: Boundary audit mechanism is fully implemented with before/after snapshots, mutation detection, BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blocker injection, and PRD blocking. Evidence agents cite project paths with line numbers. Tests verify tracked and untracked mutation detection. Missing items are gap items, not blockers.
